column1: CP - Apply Kubernetes ingress changes without rebuilding Docker images
column33:
*Description*

The CI/CD pipeline rebuilds and redeploys all affected Docker images on every push, including when only Kubernetes ingress YAML files are modified. Since ingress changes are pure infrastructure config, they do not require a Docker build, causing unnecessary pipeline runs and wasted build time.

A dedicated job was implemented to detect and apply ingress changes independently of the service build pipeline.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Changes to {{k8s/ingresses-*/**}} files trigger ingress apply without rebuilding Docker images-
* -Ingress changes to {{develop}} are applied to the dev cluster automatically-
* -Ingress changes to {{stage}} are applied to the stage cluster automatically-
* -Concurrent ingress applies across parallel deploy jobs do not cause race conditions-

*Deliverables*

column1: CP -  Restrict CORS — remove open origins from Nginx Ingress
column33:
*Description*

All backend API services currently have {{cors-allow-origin: "*"}} configured at the Nginx Ingress level, allowing requests from any origin. This is a critical security vulnerability — any external website can make authenticated cross-origin requests to the API on behalf of a logged-in user, exposing sensitive legal and financial data.

CORS enforcement should be moved to the application layer with a strict allowlist of trusted origins.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Identify where CORS is currently configured and which layer should own it-
* -Remove open CORS configuration from Nginx Ingress-
* -Implement strict origin allowlist at the application level covering all environments (dev, stage, prod)-
* -Verify that requests from unauthorized origins are blocked-
* -Verify that requests from authorized origins continue to work-

*Deliverables*

[https://github.com/cordoba-tech/cordoba-consumer-portal-backend/pull/96|https://github.com/cordoba-tech/cordoba-consumer-portal-backend/pull/96|smart-link]

column1: CP - 3/3 - Implement API Rate Limiting via Nginx Ingress
column33:
*Description* 

The backend APIs currently have no rate limiting, leaving them exposed to abuse. A multi-layer approach was evaluated (Nginx Ingress, AWS WAF, application middleware). For this iteration the focus is Nginx Ingress.

*Acceptance Criteria*

* -Rate limit thresholds defined per service-
* -Nginx rate limiting annotations added to all backend ingress files across dev, stage, and prod-
* -Validated by testing 429 responses under load-
* -Burst traffic behavior verified-

*Deliverables*

[https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CL/pages/465895426/Rate+Limiting+Nginx+Ingress+2026|https://hiredexperts.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/CL/pages/465895426/Rate+Limiting+Nginx+Ingress+2026|smart-link] 

[https://github.com/cordoba-tech/cordoba-consumer-portal-backend/pull/86|https://github.com/cordoba-tech/cordoba-consumer-portal-backend/pull/86|smart-link]

*Rate Limit Status Code — 429 vs 503*

By default, the NGINX Ingress Controller returns {{503 Service Unavailable}} when a request is rejected due to rate limiting. While functional, {{503}} is semantically incorrect — it implies the service is down, not that the client sent too many requests.
The correct status code for rate limiting is {{429 Too Many Requests}} (RFC 6585), which clearly communicates to the client that it has exceeded the allowed request rate.
*Attempted approach —* {{server-snippet}} *annotation:* The annotation {{nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/server-snippet}} allows injecting custom NGINX directives at the ingress level. We attempted to use it to set {{limit_req_status 429;}} per ingress resource. However, the NGINX Ingress Controller has {{allow-snippet-annotations}} disabled by default in recent versions for security reasons, so the admission webhook rejected the configuration.
*Final solution — ConfigMap patch:* The correct approach is to configure the NGINX Ingress Controller globally via its ConfigMap:
{noformat}kubectl patch configmap ingress-nginx-controller -n ingress-nginx1 \
  --patch '{"data":{"limit-req-status-code":"429"}}'
{noformat}
This sets {{limit_req_status 429}} at the controller level, applying to all ingresses in the cluster without requiring changes to individual ingress resources.

*Future Considerations*

* *AWS WAF* — rate-based rules at the load balancer level, before traffic reaches the cluster. Requires Terraform changes and has per-request cost. Recommended once real traffic patterns are available to calibrate rules
* *Application-level middleware (.NET + Redis)* — per-user rate limiting using {{ContactId}} from the JWT claim. More precise than IP-based limiting (handles shared IPs, VPNs). Can be implemented in {{WebApiConfigurator}} and applied to all services automatically

[https://www.civo.com/learn/rate-limiting-applications-with-nginx-ingress|https://www.civo.com/learn/rate-limiting-applications-with-nginx-ingress|smart-link] 

[https://kubernetes.github.io/ingress-nginx/user-guide/nginx-configuration/annotations/|https://kubernetes.github.io/ingress-nginx/user-guide/nginx-configuration/annotations/|smart-link] 

[https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/performance/rate-limit?view=aspnetcore-10.0|https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/performance/rate-limit?view=aspnetcore-10.0|smart-link] 

[https://testfully.io/blog/api-rate-limit/|https://testfully.io/blog/api-rate-limit/|smart-link]

column112:
19/May/26 8:45 AM;712020:fd36cadf-9806-42d0-93a9-02002d8431e5;[~accountid:712020:313307ce-3202-4da7-b5a3-adcbee32f188] i set this name format in the output of the code, this is the name format:

{{f"{acc_id}__{box_name}__{caller}__{timestamp}__{folder}.mp3"}}

{{acc_id}}: The unique API ID of the account or sub-account where the voicemail belongs.

{{box_name}}: The friendly name of the voicemail box (or the voicemail box ID if no name is set).

{{caller}}: The phone number of the person who left the voicemail ({{caller_id_number}}). If the API doesn't provide one, it defaults to {{'unknown'}}.

{{timestamp}}: A Linux epoch timestamp (a long string of numbers representing the exact second the message was recorded).

{{folder}}: The status/folder of the message (usually {{new}}, {{saved}}, or {{deleted}}).

{{.mp3}}: The hardcoded file extension.



this info is saved in CouchDB a document oriented database but the access is thought API request.

column1: CX Android: Implement 401/token expiry interceptor to force re-authentication
column33:
API calls that fail due to an expired session token are not currently handled. An interceptor (or equivalent) should detect token expiry errors from any endpoint response and redirect the user to the login screen, clearing any local session state in the process.



*ACs:*

* -Stop closing the app and start redirecting to Login-
* -A request/response interceptor is implemented that inspects all API endpoint responses-
* -When a response indicates token expiry (e.g. {{401 Unauthorized}} with a token expiry reason), the interceptor triggers the logout flow-
* -All local session state (token, user data, etc.) is cleared upon logout-
* -The user is redirected to the login screen after session state is cleared-
* -Any in-flight or queued requests are cancelled when token expiry is detected-
